text.title: Acceptance Criteria Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Acceptance Criteria enforces.

rule.statement: Each criterion uses Given-When-Then OR rule-based checklist format; mixed formats per story are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed formats produce ambiguous test mapping.

rule.statement: Each criterion maps to ≥1 test_case_id in the test plan. Untraceable AC are rejected.
Story-level coverage MUST include: happy path, ≥1 alternative, ≥1 negative / boundary, ≥1 non-functional (perf or a11y).
rule.rationale: Happy-path-only AC pass tests but fail real users.

rule.statement: AC body contains no TBD / TODO / FIXME / 'as appropriate'. Each clause is concrete and falsifiable.
rule.rationale: Hand-waving AC pass review but fail QA.

rule.statement: Each AC set names author (full name) + version_tag (semver) + last_reviewed ISO date.
